methods.description: This method describes the process of analyzing microplastic particles from sediment samples collected in Lake Kallavesi. It involves sample preparation, density separation, visual inspection, and FTIR spectral analysis to identify and quantify microplastic particles.

steps.description: The A-sample was opened and handled only in the clean laboratory under laminar flow hood or vacuum hood to protect samples from contamination.
steps.searchable_summary: Handle samples in a clean laboratory environment to prevent contamination.

steps.description: Water from the trap samples was decanted carefully and filtered through 12-15 µm paper filters, and the filters were stored on petri dishes.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter water samples through 12-15 µm paper filters and store filters.

steps.description: The sediments were rinsed to 50 ml polypropylene (PP) centrifuge tubes using ultrapure water.
steps.searchable_summary: Rinse sediments into PP centrifuge tubes using ultrapure water.

steps.description: Excess water was filtered and stored after centrifuging the samples (3000 rpm, 10 min, brake rate of 6).
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge samples and filter excess water.

steps.description: Clastic lithic matter was separated from lighter fraction by density separation with heavy liquid (lithium heteropolytungstate, LST fast float, diluted in ultrapure water to density of 2.0 g ml −1).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform density separation using LST fast float.

steps.description: The density separation was repeated three times. Each time, the supernatant was filtered through 12-15 µm paper filters and stored in sealed petri dishes.
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat density separation and filter supernatant.

steps.description: Samples on the paper filters were visually inspected using a stereo microscope, and microplastic-appearing particles were selected and hand-picked using micro tweezers.
steps.searchable_summary: Visually inspect and hand-pick microplastic particles.

steps.description: The selected particles were rinsed with filtered ethanol to a 50-mL centrifuge tube.
From the tubes, the particle-ethanol suspensions were filtered to 5 µm silver membrane filters.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter particle-ethanol suspension through silver membrane filters.

steps.description: FTIR spectral maps were measured from the silver membrane filters with Agilent Cary 670/620 imaging FTIR spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure FTIR spectral maps from silver membrane filters.

steps.description: Data from FTIR was analyzed with SiMPle software to automatically count and identify MPs by calculating Pearson's correlation between each sample spectrum and reference spectra of plastics.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze FTIR data with SiMPle software to identify microplastics.
